title: TCG Day 2 | 2026 Pokémon Prague Regional Championships

p2.decklist_summary: Mega Starmie ex (Jetting Blow — 120 active + 50 bench, plus rapid-fire attack), Staryu, Dusknoir / Dusclops / Duskull (Cursed Blast — counter placement with self-KO trigger; ignores Mysterious Rocking), Mimikyu (deck has tutor lines), Munkidori, Budew, Meowth ex (1 copy — caster confirmed prized), Blood Moon Ursaluna (1 copy lead Game 2). Trainers: Lillie's Determination ×2, Buddy-Buddy Poffin, Ultra Ball ×3+, Boss's Orders, Night Stretcher, Rare Candy. Energy: Legacy Energy (caster called out attached early), basic Psychic, basic Darkness. Spirit Tomb (per pre-match coverage of João's bracket — likely tech vs Crustle). ACE SPEC: Unfair Stamp.

coin_flip.notes: Elmar mulliganed twice in Game 1 and three times in Game 2 — caster: 'João won the flip … chose to go first'

games.win_condition: prizes — Mega Kangaskhan ex 2-shot KO chain on Mega Starmie ex; final prize via Boss's Orders gust + switch energy on Duskull
games.key_recap: Caster recap: 'Mega Kangaskhan carrying Elmar through in game one with a rapid fire combo. Didn't even need to take a one hit knock out on the Mega Starmie. Just a two shot was enough.' Critical play: João used Cursed Blast + Jetting Blow combo for exact 120 KO on Mega Kanga, but had to put himself to 1 prize to do it. Elmar then bided time, built energy on a second Mega Kanga, used Boss's Orders on Duskull (gust to active) + Switch Energy + 2nd Boss's Orders to drag the second Duskull up for the final 2 prizes.

actions.effect: places 13 damage counters (130 dmg) on opp Pokémon, KOs self
actions.notes: ignores Mysterious Rocking Inability because it's NOT damage from a Pokémon's attack

actions.target: active Mega Kangaskhan ex (already at 130 dmg)
actions.notes: exact 120 = total 250 = KO'd Mega Kanga. Caster: 'Cursed Blast plus Jetting Blow combination for their exact KO 120 damage exactly enough.'

turns.notes: INFLECTION: João burnt his Dusknoir to KO Mega Kanga but pushed himself to 1 prize remaining. Elmar's prize lead became insurmountable.

actions.notes: gusted Duskull to active — buy time
energy switch + 1 more Boss's Orders to drag the second Duskull up
caster: 'three heads got rolled' — final KO for the last 2 prizes

games.win_condition: Elmar 'missed some math and lost' — caster (Elmar interview): 'in game two I also hit three heads in a row, sadly I also missed some math and lost'
games.notes: Elmar mulliganed THREE TIMES going first. João got +3 cards and a 'Hero's Cape prized' — ironic note. Critical: João retrieved Mega Starmie ex via Night Stretcher after Ultra Balling them away (caster: 'Night Stretcher to bring those straight back. Legacy energy is going to get attached'). João's Cursed Blast + Jetting Blow combo lined up multiple times, and Elmar miscounted Crustle's HP buffer in a key turn. ACE SPEC Unfair Stamp likely played here.
games.key_recap: Elmar (interview): 'I hit three heads in a row twice in the set, it's just absurd … but I also missed some math and lost' — Mega Kanga's 200-damage rapid-fire still came online but Elmar made an HP-arithmetic error.

games.win_condition: Mega Kangaskhan ex rapid-fire — third 3-heads roll closed the game
games.key_recap: Elmar (interview): 'to hit three heads in a row twice in the set, it's just absurd. I could not even believe my eyes.' Game 3 was Mega Kanga rolling 3 heads → 200 damage finish. Crustle walled in support.

key_moments.description: João used Dusknoir Cursed Blast (placeCountersOnOppThenSelfKO ability — already in engine effects.ts) to bypass Mysterious Rocking Inability and reach exact-OHKO math (130+120=250). Self-KO traded a 2-prize Dusknoir for a 3-prize Mega Kanga — net +1 prize, but João had to put himself to 1 prize remaining first.
key_moments.engine_lesson: VALIDATES the engine's Cursed Blast handler. Self-KO ability evaluation should weight 'opp prize trade' minus 'own prize trade' net delta. Currently scorePosition does this correctly. NO change needed.

key_moments.description: Elmar SLOWED DOWN after João self-KO'd Dusknoir — built energy on second Mega Kanga across 2 turns instead of immediately attacking. Used Boss's Orders to keep Duskull active rather than chasing the prize race.
key_moments.engine_lesson: scorePosition currently rewards taking prizes faster. Crustle archetype playbook should encode 'when opp is at 1-2 prizes and YOU have a wall, slow down — build the OHKO before swinging.' This is a phase-aware behavior the engine doesn't yet model.

key_moments.description: Elmar used Switch Energy + 2× Boss's Orders to drag Duskull from bench → active despite Crustle's normal retreat lock. Clever use of free retreat from an attached Switch Energy + gust combo for the lethal prize.
key_moments.engine_lesson: Engine handles Switch Energy correctly via energyPoolForCost — but Boss's Orders + Switch Energy chain (multi-step movement) is not currently a tracked pattern in ai.ts. Add to v2 sequencing: 'when prize lethal exists via retreat-then-gust, prioritize Switch Energy attachment AHEAD of attack.'

key_moments.description: Elmar lost to a 'missed math' — explicitly stated by Elmar in post-game interview. Had Mega Kanga rolling 3 heads (200 dmg) but didn't account for some HP buffer.
key_moments.engine_lesson: AI is IMMUNE to math errors — this is an inherent advantage the engine has over human play. The engine should simulate opponent math errors at lower difficulty levels (out of scope for current Phase 8 work, but worth noting for human-vs-AI difficulty tuning).

key_moments.description: Elmar (interview): 'I hit three heads in a row TWICE in the set' — Mega Kangaskhan ex rapid-fire variance carried two of three games. Single-coin-flip probability = 12.5% per attempt; hitting twice in one set ≈ 1.5%.
key_moments.engine_lesson: The Mega Kangaskhan ex 'rapid-fire combo' (presumably Mega Punch attack: flip a coin per energy attached, deal 100 per heads) is an extreme high-variance attacker. v2 ai.ts should evaluate coin-flip attacks by EXPECTED damage (50 per energy), not max — otherwise the engine over-weights variance plays. Confirm this is already correct in ai.ts damage estimator.

engine_notes: VALIDATES putCountersOnOppThenSelfKO (Cursed Blast) handler — game-decisive in Game 1.

Mega Starmie ex / Dusknoir (mega-starmie-dusknoir) is a NEW archetype not in aiArchetype.ts. Signature cards: Mega Starmie ex (Jetting Blow + rapid-fire), Staryu, Dusknoir + Dusclops + Duskull (Cursed Blast — KEY anti-Crustle counter), Munkidori, Spirit Tomb. Plays Cursed Blast as the rule-box bypass tool. Suggested archetype string: `mega-starmie-dusknoir`. Add to playbook list.

ANTI-CRUSTLE CARDS — engine should mark these in scorePosition matchup-evaluation: Cursed Blast (Dusknoir line), Tenacious Tail (Dudunsparce ex, anti-EX-board), Curse-Blast-style snipe abilities, non-EX attackers (Cynthia's Garchomp). Without one of these, the matchup vs Crustle is structurally near-unwinnable.
Mega Kangaskhan ex's rapid-fire variance is high — engine should NOT use max-damage estimator for coin-flip attacks. Verify ai.ts damage estimator uses expected value (E[heads] × per-heads damage).
